sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: [S-3] CPI AEROSTRUCTURES INC Shelf Registration Statement
feed.summary: CPI Aerostructures, Inc. has filed a shelf registration statement to offer up to $30,000,000 in various securities and a prospectus supplement to sell up to $17,000,000 of common stock through an at-the-market program with Craig-Hallum Capital Group LLC. The company, a manufacturer for aerospace and defense markets, reported a backlog of approximately $505 million as of December 31, 2025. This filing allows CPI Aerostructures flexibility in raising capital for general corporate purposes and working capital.

feed.title: Citius Pharmaceuticals subsidiary reports strong early uptake and broad payer coverage for LYMPHIR launch
feed.summary: Citius Pharmaceuticals reported strong early commercial momentum for LYMPHIR, demonstrating rapid institutional adoption with 83% of target accounts reviewing the product and broad payer coverage secured for approximately 80% of lives. The company is seeing sequential growth in orders and no reported reimbursement denials, while continuing its commercial buildout efforts.

feed.title: Citius Oncology (CTXR) details early LYMPHIR launch uptake and $400M CTCL market
feed.summary: Citius Oncology, a subsidiary of Citius Pharmaceuticals (CTXR), reported positive early commercial progress for LYMPHIR, a targeted immune therapy for relapsed or refractory cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL), estimating an initial market exceeding $400 million. The company highlighted growing formulary adoption in oncology centers, expanding payer coverage, and repeat orders, alongside advancing clinical development through academic collaborations for other lymphoma and solid tumors. Key safety concerns for LYMPHIR include capillary leak syndrome, infusion-related reactions, hepatotoxicity, visual impairment, and embryo-fetal toxicity.

feed.title: Cal Redwood 10-K: $0 Revenue, $5.05M Net Income from Trust
feed.summary: Cal Redwood reported $0 in operating revenues but a net income of $5.05 million, primarily from its trust account investments, with no per-share net income stated. The company, a blank-check firm, has focused on organizational setup and preparing for a business combination, using funds from its IPO and private placement to secure future acquisitions and operations. Its activities have been limited to organizational tasks and due diligence as it seeks a target company.

feed.title: Smart Powerr 10-K: $0.263M Revenue, Net Loss $2.9M
feed.summary: Smart Powerr (CREG) reported $0.263 million in sales for the period, driven by a new 10-year O&M contract, and incurred a net loss of $2.9 million. The company's operating loss widened to $3.016 million due to increased operating expenses. Key financial and business highlights include significant cash inflows from advance supplier collections and active financing of third-party operations.

feed.title: CRMD Financials: Revenue Breakdown, Margins & Competitor Comparison
feed.summary: This article analyzes the financial performance of CorMedix Inc (CRMD), focusing on its revenue breakdown, profitability margins, and competitive positioning. CRMD boasts a gross margin of 83.43%, an operating margin of 45.97%, and a net margin of 10.90%, alongside a Return on Equity (ROE) of 66.56%. The article also compares CRMD's market capitalization of $536.75M and its gross margin against competitors CTNM and CMPS, for which gross margin data is N/A.

feed.title: Symbiosis II sells multiple CLNN shares (NASDAQ: CLNN) in early 2026
feed.summary: A Form 144 filing indicates that Symbiosis II LLC has reported multiple proposed and executed sales of Common Stock in Clene Inc. (NASDAQ: CLNN) during January–March 2026. The filing details numerous open-market dispositions, including 31,297 shares sold on March 31, 2026, for $152,316.52 and 32,705 shares sold on January 12, 2026, for $179,867.17, with individual trade sizes and cash amounts provided for each transaction. This filing provides transparency into Symbiosis II LLC's recent trading activities in CLNN shares.

feed.title: Foreclosure complaint hits Clipper Realty (NYSE: CLPR) over $125M loan
feed.summary: Clipper Realty (CLPR) is facing a foreclosure complaint over a $125 million mortgage loan on its 250 Livingston Street property. The lender filed suit seeking a receiver and a forced sale of the Brooklyn asset due to a default notice issued on December 18, 2025, for unpaid amounts, including an estimated $6.3 million in interest and default interest as of March 31, 2026. The company is currently negotiating a Consent and Cooperation Agreement for a potential loan sale, but completion is uncertain.

feed.title: Clipper Realty Faces Legal Action Over Mortgage Default
feed.summary: Clipper Realty's subsidiary, 250 Livingston Owner LLC, has defaulted on a $125 million mortgage loan secured by its Brooklyn property, leading to a lawsuit by the lender. The lender is seeking the appointment of a receiver and the sale of the property, with $6.3 million in interest and default interest owed as of March 31, 2026. Clipper Realty is currently negotiating a consent and cooperation agreement for a potential loan sale, though the outcome remains uncertain.

feed.title: CNS PE Ratio & Valuation, Is CNS Overvalued
feed.summary: Cohen & Steers Inc (CNS) is currently considered undervalued, with a forward PE ratio of 18.05 compared to its five-year average of 22.83. The fair price for CNS is estimated to be between $63.76 and $81.88, which is above its current price of $62.55, indicating it is undervalued by 1.89%. However, its P/S ratio is -100% above the industry average, which, combined with revenue growth of -5.55%, suggests this premium might be unsustainable.

feed.title: CNS Pharmaceuticals 10-K: Net loss $15.85M, Loss per share $35.75
feed.summary: CNS Pharmaceuticals reported a net loss of $15.85 million for the year, primarily due to increased research and development spending for clinical programs. The company's operating loss totaled $15.99 million, with a loss per basic share of $35.75 based on 443,369 weighted average shares outstanding. They also raised equity in 2025 to fund operations and trials through mid-2026.

feed.title: Costco Wholesale Corporation $COST Shares Purchased by Community Financial Services Group LLC
feed.summary: Community Financial Services Group LLC increased its stake in Costco (NASDAQ:COST) by 19.2% in the fourth quarter, bringing its total holdings to 13,760 shares valued at $11.87 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Costco, while company insiders sold 5,038 shares worth approximately $4.79 million. Costco recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates with $4.58 EPS and $69.6 billion in revenue, and analysts have given the stock a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $1,039.

feed.title: Amcor supplies custom sustainable jar for Luxéol
feed.summary: Amcor has provided French hair care brand Luxéol with a customized Prima jar for its curl mask product. The new packaging features a specially developed lid in Luxéol’s signature pink and is made from over 50% post-consumer recycled polypropylene using Amcor’s CleanStream technology. This sustainable solution supports Luxéol's commitment to responsible innovation and local sourcing, while maintaining a premium aesthetic.

feed.title: DB Fitzpatrick & Co Inc Sells 16,262 Shares of Corning Incorporated $GLW
feed.summary: DB Fitzpatrick & Co Inc reduced its stake in Corning Incorporated (NYSE:GLW) by 58.5% in Q4, selling 16,262 shares. Despite this, Corning has seen significant institutional buying from firms like Capital Research Global Investors and Wellington Management Group LLP, with 69.80% institutional ownership. The company reported strong Q4 earnings, beating estimates with $0.72 EPS and revenue up 13.9% year-over-year, and has received several analyst price target upgrades due to its role in AI and data center infrastructure.

feed.title: NTSB Finds Ford's BlueCruise Driver Assistance System At Fault For Two Fatal Collisions
feed.summary: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) has attributed two fatal collisions in early 2024 to Ford's BlueCruise driver assistance system, citing its failure to stop for stationary vehicles and detect driver disengagement. The NTSB is calling for stronger safety standards, better oversight, and improvements to crash data recording and driver monitoring systems for partial automation technologies. This comes as Ford plans to offer "eyes-off" technology by 2028 and amid an ongoing NHTSA investigation into Tesla's Full Self-Driving system.

feed.title: HSBC upgrades Bank of America stock rating on valuation
feed.summary: HSBC has upgraded Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) to a Buy rating from Hold, citing attractive valuation and expected profitability expansion. Despite lowering the price target to $55 from $57, the firm anticipates healthy revenue and earnings growth as fixed-rate assets re-price. The bank's stock has declined year-to-date, but strong share buybacks and consistent dividend increases highlight its financial health, with the new price target suggesting a 16% upside.

feed.title: Fifth Third Wealth Advisors LLC Grows Stake in Vistra Corp. $VST

(NYSE:VST) during the fourth quarter, growing its holdings by 217.4% to 33,313 shares valued at $5.374 million. feed.title: DB Fitzpatrick & Co Inc Takes Position in Garmin Ltd. $GRMN

feed.title: Fifth Third Wealth Advisors LLC Sells 11,209 Shares of Procter & Gamble Company (The) $PG

feed.title: State Street Corp. Stock: Leadership Transition and Dividend Stability Signal Steady Path for Invest
feed.summary: State Street Corp. is reinforcing its financial leadership with the appointment of C. Jack Read as the new Chief Accounting Officer, effective August 2026. This strategic move, coupled with the confirmation of a stable quarterly dividend of $0.84, signals a steady and reliable path for investors. The article highlights State Street's strong core business model, competitive position in asset management, and its appeal to North American investors seeking defensive qualities and consistent income.

feed.title: Cambium Networks (NASDAQ: CMBM) delays 2025 10‑K, restates 2022–23 results
feed.summary: Cambium Networks (NASDAQ: CMBM) announced a delay in filing its 2025 Form 10-K and will restate financial results for 2022 and 2023, along with several quarterly statements from 2023-2024. This delay is due to errors related to variable consideration under ASC 606, including estimates for sales returns and customer rebates. The company also anticipates disclosing substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern in upcoming annual reports.

feed.title: CORE MOLDING (NYSE: CMT) EVP has 812 vested shares withheld for taxes
feed.summary: Stephanie Pulliam, CORE MOLDING TECHNOLOGIES INC's EVP of Human Resources, had 812 shares of common stock withheld for tax obligations upon the vesting of restricted stock. These were not open-market sales but rather a routine administrative event as confirmed by a Form 4 filing, leaving her with 18,333 directly held shares. The transactions involved two lots of shares at prices of $18.77 and $19.15 per share respectively.

feed.title: Core Molding (NYSE: CMT) EVP updates Form 3/A insider holdings
feed.summary: Core Molding Technologies Inc.'s EVP, Stephanie Pulliam, filed an amended Form 3/A to correct her insider share holdings. The amendment removes performance-based shares that were mistakenly included in her initial filing on March 13, 2026. Following this correction, Pulliam directly holds 14,299 shares of common stock, clarifying her ownership position without indicating any new buy or sell transactions.

feed.title: Chegg moves director to different class to rebalance board composition
feed.summary: Chegg Inc. announced that director Renee Budig resigned from her Class I board position and was immediately re-elected as a Class III director on March 25, 2026. This reclassification was done to ensure equal composition across the company's board classes, moving her term expiration from June 2026 to the 2028 annual meeting. The company stated this move preserves her continuous board service and allows stockholders to vote on her re-election once every three years.

feed.title: Ciena Shareholders Back Board, Auditor and Executive Pay
feed.summary: Ciena shareholders re-elected three Class II directors, including CEO Gary B. Smith, for three-year terms at their 2026 annual meeting. They also ratified PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as the independent auditor for fiscal 2026 and approved the compensation for the company's named executive officers. These decisions reflect strong investor confidence in Ciena's governance, financial oversight, and leadership.

feed.title: Camden National (NASDAQ: CAC) sets $0.42 dividend and 3.57% yield
feed.summary: Camden National Corporation (NASDAQ: CAC) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.42 per share for its common stock, payable on April 30, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 15, 2026. This dividend represents an annualized yield of 3.57%, based on the March 30, 2026 closing price of $47.03 per share. The company, Northern New England's largest publicly traded bank holding company, issued a press release and filed an 8-K report detailing this event.

feed.title: Camden National declares $0.42 quarterly dividend

feed.title: Volkswagen Unveils Sleeker, Fancier 2027 Atlas at New York Auto Show | Videos
feed.summary: Volkswagen has introduced a heavily revised, second-generation 2027 Atlas at the New York Auto Show, eight years after the original model debuted. The new Atlas boasts a sleeker, more luxurious design with a heavily revised platform, updated styling, and a much nicer interior featuring premium materials and advanced infotainment. Mechanically, it's powered by a revised turbocharged 2.0-liter four-cylinder engine with increased horsepower, promising better fuel economy.

feed.title: Want to Avoid Fender Benders? Study Shows You Need This Feature, But Not All Cars Have It
feed.summary: A new study by the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety (IIHS) highlights the significant benefits of modern safety technologies, particularly rear automatic emergency braking, in preventing fender benders and reducing insurance claims. While not universally standard, this feature is becoming more common across various car models. The article provides a comprehensive list of sub-$50,000 cars from the 2026 model year that include standard rear automatic emergency braking.

feed.title: Foster & Motley Inc. Reduces Stock Position in EOG Resources, Inc. $EOG

feed.title: Beyond Meat Q4 Earnings Call Highlights
feed.summary: Beyond Meat's Q4 2025 earnings show continued weakness in the plant-based meat category, with revenue down 19.7% to $61.6 million. Despite a GAAP net income of $409.9 million driven by debt restructuring, the company faced significant operating losses and reported an adjusted EBITDA loss of $69 million. Beyond Meat is strategically repositioning as "Beyond" to enter adjacent protein categories and address cost and margin issues, but also disclosed new material weaknesses in internal controls and a delayed 10-K filing.

feed.title: BTAI SEC Filings - Bioxcel Therapeutics Inc 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K Forms

Stock Titan enhances this access with AI-powered summaries, impact scoring, and sentiment analysis for each filing, helping users understand complex regulatory documents and track the company's financial and clinical developments. The latest filing, from July 21, 2025, details BioXcel's pre-sNDA submission to the FDA for expanding IGALMI's label for at-home use, with a scheduled meeting on August 20, 2025.
